cards.title: 1. Define meeting goal & outcome
cards.body: What is the single most important outcome of this meeting? Write it down before you start.

cards.title: 2. Choose meeting strategy
cards.body: What format fits best — decision, alignment, brainstorm, or update? Choose the right structure.

cards.title: 3. Identify required participants
cards.body: Who must be in the room? Remove anyone who is not essential to the outcome.

cards.title: 4. Gather required information
cards.body: What data, documents, or context do participants need? Distribute in advance.

cards.title: 5. Prepare agenda & structure
cards.body: List the key topics with time allocations. Share the agenda before the meeting.

cards.title: 6. Run discussion & decide
cards.body: Facilitate focused discussion. Drive toward a clear decision or outcome before closing.

cards.title: 7. Capture decisions, actions & minutes
cards.body: Document what was decided, who owns each action, and by when. Send to all participants.

cards.title: 1. Define objective
cards.body: What exactly needs to be accomplished? State it in one clear sentence.

cards.title: 2. Define expected output
cards.body: What does the finished result look like? Be specific about format, content, and quality.

cards.title: 3. Define success criteria
cards.body: How will you know the task is done well? List measurable criteria.

cards.title: 4. Provide context & inputs
cards.body: What background information, data, or resources does the person need to execute?

cards.title: 5. Clarify scope & assumptions
cards.body: What is in scope and what is not? State any assumptions explicitly.

cards.title: 6. Define timeline & checkpoints
cards.body: When is the deadline? Are there intermediate checkpoints or reviews?

cards.title: 7. Confirm understanding → capture task
cards.body: Have the person restate the task in their own words. Capture it in your task system.

cards.title: 1. Revisit goals & priorities
cards.body: Review your current goals and top priorities. Are they still the right ones?

cards.title: 2. Review objective results
cards.body: What did you actually complete this week? Measure against your planned outcomes.

cards.title: 3. Identify wins & leverage
cards.body: What went well? What created the most value? How can you do more of it?

cards.title: 4. Identify friction & mistakes
cards.body: What slowed you down or went wrong? What would you do differently?

cards.title: 5. Improve systems & habits
cards.body: What process, habit, or tool can you improve to perform better next week?

cards.title: 6. Check strategic alignment
cards.body: Is your weekly work aligned with your long-term goals? Adjust if needed.

cards.title: 7. Define next week priorities & capture tasks
cards.body: Set your top 3 priorities for next week. Capture all open tasks and next actions.

cards.title: 1. Define the decision question
cards.body: State the decision as a clear, specific question. Ambiguity leads to poor decisions.

cards.title: 2. Define success criteria
cards.body: What does a good outcome look like? Define criteria before evaluating options.

cards.title: 3. Identify decision owner
cards.body: Who has final authority? Clarify accountability before the discussion begins.

cards.title: 4. List options
cards.body: Generate at least 3 realistic options. Include the option of doing nothing.

cards.title: 5. Evaluate risks & upside
cards.body: For each option, assess the potential upside and the key risks or downsides.

cards.title: 6. Decide
cards.body: Choose the best option based on your criteria. Avoid over-analysis — decide and move.

cards.title: 7. Capture decision & next actions
cards.body: Document the decision, the reasoning, and the immediate next actions with owners.

cards.title: 1. Revisit long-term goal
cards.body: What is the most important goal you are working toward? Keep it front of mind.

cards.title: 2. Identify today's leverage point
cards.body: What single action today would create the most progress toward your goal?

cards.title: 3. Define the single most important outcome
cards.body: What is the one result that would make today a success? Write it down.

cards.title: 4. Remove blockers & distractions
cards.body: What could prevent you from executing? Eliminate or defer it before you start.

cards.title: 5. Execute the high-leverage task
cards.body: Do the most important task first, with full focus. Protect this time.

cards.title: 6. Capture insights or improvements
cards.body: What did you learn today? What would you do differently? Note it immediately.

cards.title: 7. Prepare tomorrow's focus
cards.body: Define tomorrow's single most important outcome before you close today.

cards.title: 1. Define the real problem
cards.body: What is actually wrong? Separate symptoms from root causes. State the problem precisely.

cards.title: 2. Form initial hypothesis
cards.body: What do you believe is causing the problem? State your best guess as a testable hypothesis.

cards.title: 3. Break problem into components
cards.body: Decompose the problem into its key parts. Which component is most critical to solve?

cards.title: 4. Gather critical evidence
cards.body: What data or information do you need to validate or disprove your hypothesis?

cards.title: 5. Test the hypothesis
cards.body: Evaluate the evidence against your hypothesis. Does it hold up?

cards.title: 6. Refine or pivot
cards.body: If the hypothesis is wrong, update it and re-test. Iterate until you reach the root cause.

cards.title: 7. Decide next action & capture
cards.body: Define the solution or next step. Capture it with a clear owner and timeline.

cards.title: 1. Define the opportunity
cards.body: What exactly is the opportunity? Describe it clearly and concisely.

cards.title: 2. Define success criteria
cards.body: What does a successful outcome look like? Set measurable criteria before evaluating.

cards.title: 3. Estimate upside potential
cards.body: What is the realistic best-case outcome? Quantify value, impact, or return if possible.

cards.title: 4. Evaluate risks & costs
cards.body: What could go wrong? What resources, time, or capital are required?

cards.title: 5. Check strategic alignment
cards.body: Does this opportunity align with your current goals and priorities? Or is it a distraction?

cards.title: 6. Define go / no-go rule
cards.body: What condition must be true for you to pursue this? State it explicitly.

cards.title: 7. Decide & commit
cards.body: Make a clear decision: pursue, defer, or decline. Capture the decision and next action.

cards.title: 1. Observe the situation
cards.body: What is actually happening? Gather raw facts and data without interpretation.

cards.title: 2. Interpret what it means
cards.body: What do the observations tell you? Identify patterns, signals, and implications.

cards.title: 3. Identify possible actions
cards.body: What are your realistic options given the situation? List them without filtering.

cards.title: 4. Choose best option
cards.body: Which action best fits the situation and your goals? Decide with the information you have.

cards.title: 5. Execute
cards.body: Act decisively. Speed matters — a good decision executed now beats a perfect one too late.

cards.title: 6. Capture results
cards.body: What happened as a result of your action? Record the outcome objectively.

cards.title: 7. Adjust next step
cards.body: What does the result tell you? Update your understanding and begin the next OODA loop.
